strengths: Over 12 years of polyglot full-stack engineering with direct hands-on TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK, and Docker/Kubernetes — covering the bulk of the primary stack and cloud/containerization requirements.

Current role at re:cinq explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators for agentic workflows — a rare, direct match for the job's AI agent development requirement.

Deep, demonstrated TDD culture: authored an open-source D-language test runner (Trial) and assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), practised TDD/pairing/trunk-based development at RIDE Capital, and ran community code kata and refactoring dojo sessions.
concerns: Nest.js, Vitest, and Playwright — all listed in the primary stack or testing requirements — do not appear in the resume; ramp-up time on framework specifics should be expected.
Kotlin and Python, both listed as required multi-language proficiencies, are absent from the skills section and project history.
Explicit relational database depth is thin: MySQL appears in the skills list but PostgreSQL — the first database named in the JD — is never mentioned, leaving RDBMS breadth partially unverified.
response.justification: James is a strong fit for this role: his current position at re:cinq directly delivers AI-agent tooling and MCP framework work, hitting the most differentiating requirement on the JD, while his Optilyz tenure confirms production-grade TypeScript, React, MongoDB, and AWS at scale. The gaps — Nest.js, Kotlin, Python — are real but low-risk given 12+ years of demonstrated ability to pick up languages and frameworks across embedded, mobile, web, and now agentic domains; a candidate who built his own test runner and assertion library will not struggle with a new Node.js framework. His staff-level and founding roles, combined with a consistent TDD and code-quality ethos, suggest he can contribute to architecture decisions and code-review culture from day one.
